sources.title: AI at Work: Momentum Builds, but Gaps Remain
sources.abstract: BCG's 2025 analysis of AI adoption in the workplace, examining momentum in generative AI implementation while identifying critical gaps in enterprise AI strategies, workforce readiness, and organizational change management. Survey of 10,635 employees across 11 nations.
sources.citation_format: Boston Consulting Group. (2025). AI at Work: Momentum Builds, but Gaps Remain.
sources.description: BCG's comprehensive 2025 workplace AI survey covering 10,635 employees across 11 nations, identifying the 'silicon ceiling' phenomenon affecting frontline workers

key_findings: 72% of workers are regular Gen AI users
Silicon ceiling identified for frontline adoption

sources.title: The Widening AI Value Gap
sources.abstract: BCG's 2025 research on AI value generation, revealing that 5% of 'future-built' companies achieve 5x revenue increases and 3x cost reductions compared to laggards. Explores the emergence of agentic AI and the expanding value gap between AI leaders and followers.
sources.citation_format: Boston Consulting Group. (2025). The Widening AI Value Gap.
sources.description: BCG's empirical research proving future-built companies achieve 5x revenue increases and 3x cost reductions from AI compared to laggards

key_findings: 35% are scaling AI and generating value
AI agents account for 17% of total AI value in 2025
Future-built companies spend 26% more on IT
metadata.methodology: Global enterprise survey and financial analysis

sources.title: The State of AI: How Organizations Are Rewiring to Capture Value
sources.abstract: McKinsey's 2025 global survey of nearly 1,500 organizations examining how companies are rewiring themselves to fully capture AI value. Survey shows 78% of organizations use AI in at least one business function, with workflow redesign ranking as the #1 driver of impact.
sources.citation_format: McKinsey & Company. (2025). The State of AI: How Organizations Are Rewiring to Capture Value. QuantumBlack AI by McKinsey.
sources.description: McKinsey's flagship annual AI report surveying ~1,500 organizations globally, emphasizing workflow redesign as the #1 driver of AI impact

key_findings: 78% of organizations use AI in at least one function
71% regularly use gen AI across functions
Workflow redesign ranks as #1 driver of impact
Organizations use AI in average of 3 business functions

sources.title: Superagency in the Workplace: Empowering People to Unlock AI's Full Potential at Work
sources.abstract: McKinsey's 2025 workplace AI report examining how to empower employees with AI tools while maintaining human agency. Research sizes the long-term AI opportunity at $4.4 trillion in productivity growth potential from corporate use cases.
sources.citation_format: McKinsey & Company. (2025). Superagency in the Workplace: Empowering People to Unlock AI's Full Potential at Work.
sources.description: McKinsey's analysis of AI's $4.4 trillion productivity opportunity and how to empower employees while maintaining human agency

sources.title: Technology Vision 2025: AI - A Declaration of Autonomy
sources.abstract: Accenture's 25th annual Technology Vision report examining AI autonomy and its transformative impact. Research shows generative AI is expected to drive productivity gains of 20% in companies leading in AI adoption, with focus on agentic AI and autonomous systems.
sources.citation_format: Accenture. (2025). Technology Vision 2025: AI - A Declaration of Autonomy.
sources.description: Accenture's flagship 25th annual technology trends report focusing on AI autonomy and 20% productivity gains from generative AI

key_findings: 20% productivity gains expected from gen AI
Less than 1% of today's internet market cap founded in first 2 years after Netscape

sources.title: Making Reinvention Real with Gen AI
sources.abstract: Accenture's report on moving from AI experimentation to impact, featuring case studies including Accenture's own Marketing + Communications function using 14 specialized AI agents. Details 30% reduction in manual tasks and 25-55% improvement in speed-to-market.
sources.citation_format: Accenture. (2024). Making Reinvention Real with Gen AI.
sources.description: Accenture's case study showing 30% reduction in manual tasks and 25-55% speed-to-market improvement using 14 AI agents

sources.title: The Front-Runner's Guide to Scaling AI
sources.abstract: Accenture's 2025 point of view on how companies develop and deploy AI models to create financial and non-financial value. Based on survey fielded June-July 2024 examining AI scaling strategies and best practices.
sources.citation_format: Accenture. (2025). The Front-Runner's Guide to Scaling AI.
sources.description: Accenture's guide on AI scaling strategies based on June-July 2024 survey examining value creation

sources.title: State of Generative AI in the Enterprise: Now Decides Next - Q4 2024
sources.abstract: Deloitte AI Institute's Q4 2024 conclusion of the quarterly survey series tracking GenAI investments, successes and challenges throughout 2024. Survey of 2,773 director- to C-suite respondents across six industries and 14 countries reveals positive pragmatism replacing initial hype.
sources.series_name: Deloitte State of GenAI in Enterprise Series
sources.citation_format: Deloitte AI Institute. (2025). State of Generative AI in the Enterprise: Now Decides Next - Q4 2024.
sources.description: Deloitte's Q4 2024 GenAI enterprise survey covering 2,773 executives across 6 industries and 14 countries

key_findings: Agentic AI is on the rise

sources.title: TMT Predictions 2025: Bridging the Gaps
sources.abstract: Deloitte's 2025 Technology, Media & Telecommunications predictions identifying 2025 as a 'gap year' for generative AI. Covers eight critical gaps including infrastructure monetization, gender disparities, energy consumption, deepfake trust, and gen AI agents.
sources.citation_format: Deloitte. (2024). TMT Predictions 2025: Bridging the Gaps.
sources.description: Deloitte's prediction that 2025 will be a 'gap year' for GenAI, identifying eight critical gaps to bridge

key_findings: 2025 is a 'gap year' for gen AI
Less than 3% of production budgets for gen AI in studios
Gen AI adoption gender gap of 10-17 points in Europe

sources.title: Technology Report 2025: AI Agents and the Future of Tech
sources.abstract: Bain's sixth annual Technology Report examining AI as the defining disrupter of the decade. Report shows leaders using AI to improve EBITDA by 10-25% while exploring agentic AI implications for software strategy, enterprise deployment, robotics, and IT architecture.
sources.citation_format: Bain & Company. (2025). Technology Report 2025: AI Agents and the Future of Tech.
sources.description: Bain's 6th annual tech report showing AI leaders improving EBITDA by 10-25% with focus on agentic AI

key_findings: Agentic AI is immediate top story

sources.title: Gen AI Adoption Continues Rapid Pace
sources.abstract: Bain's December 2024 report finding 95% of US companies now using generative AI, with adoption growing 12% since October 2023. Average use cases in production doubled from 2.5 to 5, with 160 employees on average dedicating time to Gen AI (up 30%).
sources.citation_format: Bain & Company. (2025). Gen AI Adoption Continues Rapid Pace.
sources.description: Bain's survey showing 95% of US companies using GenAI with use cases doubling and annual budgets reaching $10M

key_findings: 95% of US companies using gen AI

sources.title: 2025 AI Business Predictions
sources.abstract: PwC's 2025 AI predictions covering the shift from AI adoption to AI success through vision and strategy. Survey shows 49% of tech leaders have AI fully integrated into core business strategy, with emphasis on AI choices as career-defining decisions.
sources.citation_format: PwC. (2024). 2025 AI Business Predictions.
sources.description: PwC's 2025 predictions emphasizing AI success requires vision as much as adoption, with AI agents doubling knowledge workforce

key_findings: 49% have AI fully integrated in strategy
20-30% gains in productivity and revenue at scale
AI agents could double knowledge workforce
Enterprise AI investment to reach $307B in 2025

sources.title: EY FY2025 AI Revenue Growth and Strategy
sources.abstract: EY's FY2025 results showing AI-related revenue up 30%, with $53.2 billion total revenue (4% growth). Report covers 1,000 AI agents, 100+ internal AI applications, and $1B+ annual AI investment delivering enterprise-wide transformations and AI governance frameworks.
sources.citation_format: EY. (2025). EY FY2025 Financial Results: AI Revenue Growth and Strategy.
sources.description: EY's FY2025 results showing 30% AI revenue growth, $1B+ AI investment, 1,000 AI agents deployed
